evidence: "THE COCOANUTS is historically significant as one of the first all-talking, all-singing musical films... it remains very entertaining thanks to the brothers' anarchic comedy."
"THE BLACK PIRATE has everything you could want in a seafaring adventure: cutthroat pirates, swordplay, a damsel in distress..."
"the film is really not much more than a string of burlesque routines held together by a simple plotline."
"The story has would-be detectives Stan and Ollie safeguarding a powerful bomb..."
"The gags are fast and furious and many are truly surreal..."
"...this one benefits from a lovely Jerome Kern score, and from the film debut of Bud Abbott and Lou Costello."
"the Kino source print has apparently been pieced together from the best available materials and with a few minor exceptions is sharp and clear."
"the film looks and sounds better than it has in years."

brand_loyalty.value: high for Abbott and Costello, Laurel and Hardy
evidence: "ABBOTT AND COSTELLO MEET FRANKENSTEIN gave a real boost to the comedy team's film career; it's an excellent film..."
"WAY OUT WEST ties with SONS OF THE DESERT as Laurel and Hardy's best feature film."
"the highly imaginative sets and special effects set the right note of fantasy..."
"the film has been influential to modern film makers..."
"THE MUPPET CHRISTMAS CAROL brings greater depth and humanity to the story than many recent versions."
"A magnificent cast... find all the vibrancy in Shaw's prose."

summary: The user has a strong preference for comedy and musical genres, particularly enjoying the works of Abbott and Costello and Laurel and Hardy. They appreciate fast-paced narratives with simple plots, while showing a medium sensitivity to production quality and emotional depth.
